title: Historical MMSp / cockpit kernel archive
availability: Capsule is in D-ND_LAB. Full historical prompts must be provided separately or vendored before BODY use.
use_when: Current Lab/meta-lab docs show a conceptual hole that may have been solved in older MMSp.
A named older entity such as ALAN, SACS-PS, Halo, COAC, AWO, OCC, Morpheus or AETO is directly relevant.
The system needs lineage clarification before creating a new skill or meta-prompt.
A future instance must understand why a current pattern exists.
do_not_use_for: Directly injecting old system prompts into a Lab cycle.
Treating historical language as current runtime procedure.
Overriding current validators, MML, assertions or UI contracts.
Promoting a pattern without checking against live repo behavior.

distilled_patterns.summary: Older prompts can explain origin and missing intent of current skill/lab behavior.
Named entities like ALAN, SACS-PS, Halo, COAC, OCC and Morpheus map to logic, architecture, genome, orchestration and stalling patterns.
Historical prompts must be reduced to procedure and compared with the living repo before use.

contamination_risks: Historical prompts can be semantically rich but procedurally obsolete.
Old cockpit assumptions may conflict with D-ND_LAB public install constraints.
Named entities can become persona theater if not reduced to outputs.
Lineage files may include broad system instructions not appropriate for public repo reuse.
